text.title: csharp-entity-framework Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for csharp-entity-framework as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Lazy loading via UseLazyLoadingProxies() enabled globally, followed by accessing navigation properties in loops.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lazy loading silently adds a query whenever a navigation property is accessed, causing N+1 queries. Agents enable it because tutorials do, then performance collapses.

failure-mode.description: DbContextOptions mis-registration: agents register Singleton or Transient — Scoped is the correct lifetime, AddDbContextPool is the right factory.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Non-scoped contexts are shared across requests or not reused, causing concurrency bugs or allocation overhead.

failure-mode.description: New entity but no DbSet<> on the context — code compiles, runtime LINQ throws "entity not part of model".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: EF Core tracks entities by DbSet. Missing the DbSet entry breaks runtime LINQ queries.

failure-mode.description: Modifying a deployed migration: agents "fix" a generated migration file rather than adding a new one, breaking environments that already applied it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Deployed migrations are immutable contracts. Editing breaks idempotency and produces inconsistent schemas across environments.

failure-mode.description: Update() marks every property as modified, including null nav-props; partial updates need attaching + setting EntityState.Modified on specific properties.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Update() is for full replacements, not patches. Partial updates overwrite unmodified properties with defaults.

failure-mode.description: Connection pooling (AddDbContextPool) caches contexts; mutating ChangeTracker.Tracked settings on one request leaks to the next.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pooled contexts are reused across requests. State set on one request carries over to the next unless explicitly reset.

failure-mode.description: EnableSensitiveDataLogging() enabled without gating on IsDevelopment(), leaking PII to prod logs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sensitive data logging is a security regression. It must be development-only.
